name: Shanghai Red Cooked Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Peanut (or vegetable) oil for braising
recipeInstructions:
Red-cooking is a form of stewing with a seasoned liquid containing soy sauce and spices. If serving more people, get more chickens. Chickens should not exceed about 4 lbs each.
Marinating: Wash chicken thoroughly under cool water, then dry. Cut off fat pockets around cavity opening. Place chicken in a snug bowl. Brush or rub dark soy sauce into skin; let stand in bowl for 30 minutes; repeat brushing several times. Remove from bowl.
Braising: In wok, heat oil for braising over medium-high flame until hot but not smoking. Quickly lower chicken into oil. Protect yourself against splattering oil by holding the wok lid over the wok; do not actually cover it. Using a large spoon, baste chicken with hot oil and turn once or twice so skin browns evenly (about 10-15 minutes). Carefully remove chicken from wok, trying not to break the skin.
Stewing: Chop green onions into 2-inch sections. Heat peanut oil in a heavy pot or Dutch oven that fits snugly around the chicken. When oil is hot, add green onions and ginger. Saute until onions are lightly browned. Add water, sherry, thin soy sauce, star anise, and salt. Bring mixture to boil.
While liquid is heating, loosely tie legs of chicken together with a piece of twine. Lower chicken into boiling liquid. Bring liquid to boil again, then reduce heat for a very slow simmer. Chicken should float freely in the liquid. Cover and simmer for about 1 hour or until chicken is very tender. Leave chicken in covered pot for 5 minutes before serving. Then remove it carefully to a serving platter; remove twine. Remove all but 1 cup of liquid from the pot (including onion pieces). Bring remaining liquid to boil, thicken slightly with cornstarch paste, and glaze with sesame oil. Pour sauce over chicken and serve.
